summary: 12 items surfaced from 24 stories; 1 product skipped on cooldown.

items.name: BBEdit
items.summary: A Mac text editor update; commenters praised shell-script hooks and the usable free tier.
items.why_included: It drew unusually specific workflow praise, not just nostalgia.
items.evidence: Comments called it extensible with shell scripts, Python tools, and Rust apps, and noted the free version is very usable.

items.name: Runtime
items.source_story_title: Launch HN: Runtime (YC P26) – Sandboxed coding agents for everyone on a team
items.summary: Sandboxed coding agents for teams, with commenters comparing Claude Code and open-source alternatives.
items.why_included: The thread surfaced real product comparisons instead of generic launch hype.
items.evidence: People compared it to Claude Code and linked open-source alternatives like smithy-ai and norisessions.

items.name: AgentMail
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Agent.email – sign up via curl, claim with a human OTP
items.summary: An email inbox API for agents; the launch centers on curl sign-up and human OTP flows.
items.why_included: It has a crisp use case and immediate implementation details.
items.evidence: The launch thread talked about agents hitting agent.email via curl and handing off a human OTP to claim the inbox.

items.name: Python 3.15
items.source_story_title: Python 3.15: features that didn't make the headlines
items.summary: A roundup of lesser-known 3.15 changes, with concrete iterator and packaging links.
items.why_included: The comments pointed to real release notes and packages rather than vague language debate.
items.evidence: Commenters linked the official 3.15 notes and a PyPI package, threaded-generator, built around the new behavior.

items.name: Git commits as folders via NFS
items.source_story_title: Mounting git commits as folders with NFS (2023)
items.summary: A filesystem trick for browsing git history as folders; commenters compared ClearCase and Fossil fusefs.
items.why_included: The thread turned into a concrete tooling comparison with working alternatives.
items.evidence: People invoked ClearCase's NFS-backed browsing and Fossil's fusefs subcommand.

items.name: Gemma 4 31B local video indexing
items.source_story_title: Indexing a year of video locally on a 2021 MacBook with Gemma4-31B (50GB swap)
items.summary: A MacBook pipeline for indexing a year of video locally with Gemma 4 31B.
items.why_included: The comments dug into models, sidecar files, and local tooling details.
items.evidence: People discussed Gemma 4 31B quantization, Unsloth Studio, and the plain-text .description.md file layout.

items.name: RTX 6000 Pro home GPU server
items.source_story_title: Was my $48K GPU server worth it?
items.summary: A $48K GPU server benchmarked against newer RTX 6000 Pro setups.
items.why_included: The thread stayed grounded in actual hardware choices and price/perf tradeoffs.
items.evidence: Commenters compared the RTX 6000 Ada 48GB with the RTX 6000 Pro 96GB and called the newer card the sweet spot.

items.name: Freenet
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Freenet, a peer-to-peer platform for decentralized apps
items.summary: A peer-to-peer anonymity platform returning with a rewrite and service model.
items.why_included: The discussion centered on the concrete rewrite architecture, not abstract decentralization.
items.evidence: A commenter called it a favorite project and others discussed anonymity services and a related talk/video.

items.name: Vivaldi
items.summary: A browser update praised for Workspaces and a sustainable business model.
items.why_included: Multiple commenters gave direct adoption stories and feature praise.
items.evidence: People said they switched to Vivaldi for its Workspaces and recommended it as a browser with a sustainable model.

items.name: Slop Grenade
items.source_story_title: Throwing AI-generated walls of text into conversations
items.summary: A small site for pushing back on AI-generated wall-of-text spam.
items.why_included: The thread latched onto the brand and pointed to adjacent tools and sites.
items.evidence: Commenters praised the slop grenade branding and linked stopsloppypasta.ai plus Kagi Translate's proofreading feature.

items.name: Curta
items.summary: The classic pocket calculator, still treated as the benchmark collector's piece.
items.why_included: The comments were full of direct admiration rather than generic museum chatter.
items.evidence: People said they dreamed of owning a Curta and called it the ultimate calculator to own.

items.name: Project Hail Mary star chart
items.source_story_title: Project Hail Mary – Stellar Navigation Chart
items.summary: An interactive stellar navigation chart built around GAIA DR3 data.
items.why_included: The discussion anchored the chart in specific videos and astronomy datasets.
items.evidence: Commenters pointed to a related YouTube visualization and noted that the demo uses ESA's GAIA DR3 dataset.
